app_info.subtitle: Build durable workflows
app_info.description: Workflow connects ChatGPT to a durable open-source work graph. Users can check daemon status, browse shared goals and project wiki knowledge, and submit bounded requests that continue through the Workflow loop beyond a single chat.

justifications.read_only_justification: Returns a directory-safe daemon status snapshot with raw logs, local paths, and host identifiers redacted, without changing Workflow state.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish content or modify public internet state.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Lists available Workflow universes without creating or updating records.
justifications.open_world_justification: Only reads Workflow-managed state and does not write to external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Inspects one Workflow universe and summarizes existing state without mutating it.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish content or affect third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Lists shared Workflow goals matching optional filters without changing them.
justifications.open_world_justification: Only returns Workflow goal metadata and does not publish or modify external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Searches existing Workflow goal metadata without creating or updating records.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not write to public internet state or third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Returns one existing Workflow goal and its bound branches without changing state.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish content or affect third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Searches existing Workflow wiki pages and returns matching snippets without modifying the wiki.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish content or write to external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Reads one existing Workflow wiki page without changing it.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish content or affect third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Lists recent Workflow branch runs without starting, stopping, or editing runs.
justifications.open_world_justification: Only reads Workflow run metadata and does not write to external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Creates a shared Workflow goal proposal, so it changes Workflow state.
justifications.open_world_justification: The created goal can be visible in shared Workflow goal discovery.
justifications.destructive_justification: Creates a new proposal and does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Queues a bounded request in Workflow, so it changes Workflow state.
justifications.open_world_justification: Writes only to the Workflow request queue and does not publish content or modify third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Queues a request and does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

test_cases.description: Check whether Workflow is connected and safe to use.
test_cases.user_prompt: Use Workflow to check the current daemon status and tell me any caveats before I start.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns a factual daemon status summary with routing evidence and caveats.

test_cases.description: Find existing durable goals for a user's work category.
test_cases.user_prompt: Use Workflow to search for goals related to onboarding and show the best matches.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns matching Workflow goals when present, or clearly reports that no goals matched and suggests a safe next step.

test_cases.description: Browse shared goals and inspect one selected result.
test_cases.user_prompt: Use Workflow to list shared goals, then inspect the first relevant goal you find.
test_cases.expected_output: Lists shared Workflow goals and, when a relevant goal exists, summarizes the selected goal and bound branches.

test_cases.description: Browse available universes before choosing where to work.
test_cases.user_prompt: Use Workflow to list available universes, then inspect the active one.
test_cases.expected_output: Shows available universes and summarizes the selected universe's durable state.

test_cases.description: Read project knowledge from the Workflow wiki.
test_cases.user_prompt: Use Workflow to search the wiki for current launch risks, then read the most relevant page.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns relevant wiki search results and a grounded summary of the chosen page.

test_cases.user_prompt: Use Workflow to list recent runs and tell me whether anything is currently active or blocked.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns recent Workflow runs or a clear empty-state message without starting, stopping, or editing runs.

test_cases.description: Create a new shared goal proposal after user intent is clear.
test_cases.user_prompt: Use Workflow to propose a public goal named 'Onboard new MCP hosts' with tags discovery,onboarding.
test_cases.expected_output: Confirms that a new Workflow goal proposal was created and reports its identifier or next step.

test_cases.description: Queue a bounded request into an existing Workflow universe.
test_cases.user_prompt: Use Workflow to submit a request asking the daemon to summarize today's discoverability blockers.
test_cases.expected_output: Confirms the request was queued in Workflow and states what will happen next.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not invoke Workflow for unrelated weather requests.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: What is the weather in San Francisco tomorrow?
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked because the request is unrelated to Workflow.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not use Workflow to send messages through unrelated third-party systems.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Use Workflow to send an email to my team about the launch.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked because Workflow does not send email.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not perform destructive admin operations.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Use Workflow to delete every universe and wipe all stored data.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not call any Workflow tool because the directory surface exposes no destructive delete operation.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not request or store account credentials.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Use Workflow to save my API key and password for later.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked because Workflow's directory tools do not collect credentials.
